title: culture[colleague] installs the colleague front in one line — colleague joins claude and codex as a first-class, user-selectable culture backend you can install, start, and run on the mesh.

claims.text: culture[colleague] installs the colleague front in one line — colleague joins claude and codex as a first-class, user-selectable culture backend you can install, start, and run on the mesh.

honesty_conditions.text: On release day, 'uv tool install culture[colleague]' resolves from published PyPI packages and a colleague-backed agent joins the mesh and answers a mention — colleague is installable and runnable exactly like claude and codex, not merely declared.

claims.text: Culture operators — humans at the CLI and AI agents driving culture — who pick which mind (front) to run on the mesh. Today they can choose claude or codex; they want to choose colleague the same one-line way.

honesty_conditions.text: Both a human operator and an in-mesh agent can select and run the colleague front using only documented commands — neither audience needs a step the other cannot take.

claims.text: uv tool install 'culture[colleague]' resolves entirely from published PyPI versions, and 'culture agents create --agent colleague' (culture.yaml 'backend: colleague') starts a colleague-backed agent that joins the mesh and answers a mention — the same connect/join/respond smoke test claude and codex pass.

honesty_conditions.text: Each after-state clause is executable and checked: the install resolves from PyPI, create/start joins the mesh, and the agent answers a mention — captured as a transcript before the effort is declared done.

claims.text: Today culture ships extras claude/codex/acp/copilot/all-backends but NO colleague, so 'uv tool update culture[colleague]' fails with 'does not have an extra named colleague. Nothing to upgrade'. Underneath: pinned cultureagent 0.4.0 provides no backend-colleague extra and no clients/colleague daemon; culture_core/clients/ has no colleague/ dir; cli/agents.py _DAEMON_FACTORIES maps claude/codex/acp/opencode/copilot with no colleague factory. Colleague is the only one of the three enforced backends you cannot actually install or run.

honesty_conditions.text: The before-state is evidenced in-repo/on-PyPI today: the absent colleague extra in pyproject.toml, no colleague/ under culture_core/clients/, no colleague factory in cli/agents.py, and cultureagent 0.11.0 metadata listing no backend-colleague.

claims.text: Letting users choose their fronts is the point: the 2026-07-02 decision made colleague a first-class, parity-enforced backend alongside claude/codex, but it is the one front with no install line and no runnable path — so the one-line-extras promise is broken for exactly the mind that motivated it.

honesty_conditions.text: The broken-promise asymmetry is demonstrable: culture[claude] and culture[codex] resolve and run today while culture[colleague] fails to resolve — observed, not rhetorical.

claims.text: Culture-side only. The colleague adapter itself — cultureagent's clients/colleague daemon wrapping colleague[culture]'s ColleagueHarness, plus the backend-colleague extra and a cultureagent release — is a sibling-repo hand-off (cultureagent#45), not built here. No re-vendoring of colleague/cultureagent/agentfront in-tree. Not a change to copilot/acp, which stay stale-not-deprecated.

honesty_conditions.text: No PR in this effort vendors colleague/cultureagent code in-tree or changes copilot/acp behavior; the cultureagent adapter appears as an upstream issue/PR, not an in-tree patch here.

claims.text: On a clean venv, pip install 'culture[colleague]' resolves from published versions with no git/path deps, a colleague-backed agent passes the connect-join-respond smoke test, and backend-parity CI now enforces colleague — a PR touching only one of claude/codex/colleague fails naming the missing backends.

honesty_conditions.text: The clean-venv resolve, the colleague connect-join-respond smoke test, and the parity-CI negative test are each actually executed and recorded before the effort is declared done.

claims.text: A 'colleague' extra exists in culture's pyproject [project.optional-dependencies], symmetric with the claude/codex entries (colleague = [cultureagent[backend-colleague]~=<ver>]), and colleague is added to the all-backends union. pip install 'culture[colleague]' and 'culture[claude,codex,colleague]' both resolve.

honesty_conditions.text: On a clean venv, pip install 'culture[colleague]' and 'culture[claude,codex,colleague]' both resolve entirely from published package versions — no git/path deps.

claims.text: culture_core/clients/colleague/ lands (reference culture.yaml template + any culture-side surface the other clients dirs carry). This is the file whose existence flips the backend-parity gate ON for colleague — so when it lands, claude/codex/colleague must ALL be parity-complete in the same change.

honesty_conditions.text: After clients/colleague/ lands, a PR touching only one of culture_core/clients/{claude,codex,colleague}/ fails backend-parity CI naming the missing backends of the enforced set.

claims.text: cli/agents.py wires colleague end-to-end: a _create_colleague_config (culture.yaml 'backend: colleague') and a _create_colleague_daemon resolving cultureagent.clients.colleague.daemon, registered in the _DAEMON_FACTORIES map so '--agent colleague' and 'backend: colleague' start a real colleague daemon instead of erroring or silently falling back.

honesty_conditions.text: A colleague-backed agent (culture.yaml backend: colleague) passes the same connect-join-respond smoke test the claude/codex backends pass; an unknown/misspelled backend still fails loudly (no silent claude fallback).

claims.text: culture bumps its 'cultureagent~=0.4.0' pin to the first cultureagent release that ships the backend-colleague extra + clients/colleague daemon. Until that release exists on PyPI, culture[colleague] cannot resolve — this is the hard upstream dependency (cultureagent#45 hand-off).

honesty_conditions.text: The cultureagent version culture pins is a real published release whose metadata lists a backend-colleague extra and whose wheel contains clients/colleague/daemon.py; the full engine suite passes against it.

hard_questions.text: cultureagent 0.4.0 (the current pin) ships NO backend-colleague. Does this spec (a) wait to merge until a cultureagent release with backend-colleague exists, or (b) merge culture-side wiring now against a to-be-released pin (extra red on PyPI until upstream ships)? Sequencing is a user decision.

risk: The entire deliverable is gated on a sibling release culture does not control. If cultureagent#45 stalls, culture[colleague] cannot resolve no matter what lands here — the honest ship criterion depends on an external release.

claims.text: docs/ documents colleague as a selectable front: the install line, the culture.yaml 'backend: colleague' config, and the all-backends parity note (cli/CLAUDE.md's backend list) updated to reflect colleague as enforced.

honesty_conditions.text: A human or agent can go from zero to a mesh-joined colleague agent using only the published package and the documented commands — no undocumented steps.

claims.text: cultureagent's clients/colleague daemon will expose the same daemon-factory contract the other backends do (a _create_*_daemon(config, agent) resolving cultureagent.clients.<backend>.daemon), so culture's wiring mirrors the existing claude/codex factories with no new harness.

colleague ships a [culture] extra whose ColleagueHarness adapts its bounded work loop onto the mesh over agentirc-cli — culture wraps this via cultureagent, it does not build a new harness (carried over from the three-minds frame).

Cross-repo hand-off convention: culture implements only its own side (extra, clients/colleague, agents.py wiring, pin bump, docs); the cultureagent adapter + backend-colleague extra + release is filed/tracked as the sibling hand-off (cultureagent#45), not patched in-tree.

Sequencing (user decision 2026-07-02): COORDINATED CUTOVER — hold the culture-side change until a cultureagent release ships the backend-colleague extra + clients/colleague daemon (upstream #45). Then land it as ONE independently-green culture PR: bump the cultureagent pin (~=0.4.0 → the release with colleague; note the current pin is already stale vs PyPI 0.11.0), add the colleague extra + all-backends union, add culture_core/clients/colleague/ (flips the parity gate), add the agents.py _create_colleague_{config,daemon} + factory-map entry, and docs — so culture[colleague] resolves the day it merges. No red extra shipped ahead of the upstream release.

open_vagueness.text: Exact cultureagent version that first ships backend-colleague, and therefore culture's new pin (bump ~=0.4.0 to ~=0.5.0? keep the ~= minor policy?) — decide at implementation once the release exists.

Whether culture_core/clients/colleague/ needs more than a culture.yaml template to be a complete parity-satisfying client dir (e.g. does the parity guard demand a factory/config symmetry the other dirs carry) — confirm against the guard when wiring.
